# 01 — Wire AES-256-GCM encryption on WS frames and state snapshots

Status: ready-for-agent
Labels: enhancement

*This was generated by AI during triage.*

## What

`src/lib/crypto.ts` already implements AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt (random IV prepended, 12-byte IV + ciphertext layout). It is imported nowhere. All Yjs binary frames sent over WebSocket and all state snapshots stored via `PUT /api/sessions/{id}/state` go as plaintext. The server can read every byte of every document.

## Requirements

### WebSocket (outgoing)
- Before calling `provider.send()` or in the y-websocket message interceptor, encrypt the binary Yjs frame with the session key using `encrypt(frame, key)`
- The key comes from the URL fragment (`#key`) — see issue `e2e-encryption/02` which handles key generation and import

### WebSocket (incoming)
- On receiving a binary WS message, decrypt before handing to Yjs: `decrypt(frame, key)`
- If decryption fails (wrong key, truncated message), log a warning and drop the frame — do not crash

### State snapshot (outgoing — `PUT /state`)
- Before `PUT /api/sessions/{id}/state`, encode the Yjs doc with `Y.encodeStateAsUpdate(doc)` then encrypt with the session key
- Send the encrypted blob as `application/octet-stream`

### State snapshot (incoming — `GET /state`)
- After fetching the blob, decrypt before calling `Y.applyUpdate(doc, plaintext)`
- If decryption fails, treat as no prior state (empty doc) and log a warning

## Context

- `src/lib/crypto.ts` — `encrypt(data: Uint8Array, key: CryptoKey)` and `decrypt(data: Uint8Array, key: CryptoKey)` are ready to use
- The CryptoKey needs to flow from wherever it is generated/imported (see issue `e2e-encryption/02`) into the WS layer and snapshot hooks
- y-websocket does not expose a per-message hook natively — the cleanest approach is to subclass or wrap the provider, or intercept at the `doc` observer level. Review y-websocket source for the right interception point before starting.

## Depends on

- `e2e-encryption/02` — key must exist before encryption can be wired
- `websocket-frontend/01` — WS provider must be connected
- `state-snapshots/01` — snapshot PUT/GET must be wired

## Files

- `src/lib/crypto.ts` (frontend repo) — no changes needed, already complete
- `src/lib/YjsContext.tsx` or a new `src/lib/encryptedWsProvider.ts` (frontend repo)
- `src/hooks/useStateSnapshot.ts` (frontend repo, new file likely)
